mechanism_narrative: A downward drift in FEDFUNDS lowers the discount rate on equities and eases financial conditions, so S&P 500 returns are positive over the next 20 trading days when policy is in an easing path.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of periods with falling FEDFUNDS, or rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE above a stressed-labor threshold signals weakening income and demand, which hurts earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 7.5% and is up at least 0.2pp from the prior month
failure_condition: If triggered observations produce positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 40% of cases, or the sign accuracy falls below 55% over a 3-year rolling window, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous weakness in RSAFS and HOUST identifies collapsing final demand and housing demand, which foreshadows lower corporate sales and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint-low RSAFS/HOUST state does not outperform either series alone and fails to produce negative 60-day SPX returns in at least 55% of episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in INDPRO when CPIAUCSL is still elevated indicates stagflationary supply stress, which compresses margins and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If low industrial production without elevated inflation does not distinguish negative 20-day SPX returns from baseline, or sign accuracy falls below 55%, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A steady decline in PAYEMS after payroll recession begins signals broad labor-income deterioration, which reduces expected consumer spending and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If consecutive PAYEMS declines do not precede negative 5-day SPX returns more often than 50% of the time, or the edge disappears in a 2-year rolling test, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL accelerates while FEDFUNDS remains unusually low, real policy rates fall and inflation uncertainty rises, so equities reprice downward and S&P 500 returns are negative over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation rises above its 6-month average while FEDFUNDS stays below 1%
failure_condition: Reject if the low-rate inflation trigger does not produce negative 60-day SPX returns in at least 55% of occurrences, or if rolling Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in GDPC1 after contraction signals renewed aggregate demand and earnings recovery, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If positive GDPC1 momentum does not precede positive 60-day SPX returns in at least 55% of instances, or the effect vanishes in out-of-sample rolling tests, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: An inventory-like combination of weak INDPRO and weak RSAFS indicates simultaneous production and sales deterioration, which signals earnings compression and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO is below its 3-month average and RSAFS is below its 3-month average
failure_condition: If the joint weak-production/weak-sales state does not yield negative 20-day SPX returns in at least 55% of triggered cases, or the interaction term adds no incremental predictive value over either proxy alone, reject the hypothesis.
